articles.excerpt: Palm Sunday through Pascha, a complete guide to every service this Holy Week at Sts. Peter and Paul, from April 5th through the midnight Paschal Liturgy on April 12th.
articles.body:
Holy Week begins this Sunday with Palm Sunday Liturgy at 9:30 AM, and ends in the middle of the night on April 12th when we proclaim the Resurrection of Christ. What happens in between is the most important week of the year.

Here is what is ahead of us.

Palm Sunday, April 5, 9:30 AM. Divine Liturgy. We begin the week with the triumphant entry of Christ into Jerusalem. The crowd cries hosanna; within days they will cry crucify. Come with your palms.

Bridegroom Matins, April 6, 7, and 8, at 6:30 PM each evening. These are among the most beautiful services of the Lenten season. The service centers on the parable of the ten virgins and the image of Christ as the Bridegroom coming in the middle of the night. The refrain runs through the service: "Behold the Bridegroom comes at midnight." Come to all three if you can. Come to at least one.

Holy Unction, Wednesday April 8, 9:30 AM. The sacrament of Holy Unction is not reserved for those near death. The Church offers it to anyone seeking God's healing of body and soul, which is all of us. Seven epistle and gospel readings. Seven anointings with blessed oil. Plan to be there for the full service.

The Twelve Passion Gospels, Holy Thursday evening, April 9, at 6:30 PM. Twelve readings from all four Gospels trace the events from the Last Supper through the crucifixion and burial. The service takes about two hours. It is worth every minute. This is the night we hold candles and listen to the story of how love suffered.

Holy Friday, April 10. Two services. Royal Hours at 10:00 AM. Matins of Holy Saturday with the Lamentations at 6:30 PM. At the evening service we process around the church carrying the Epitaphios, the burial icon of Christ. This is the service when the grief is felt most directly. Come to both.

Vesperal Liturgy of St. Basil the Great, Holy Saturday, April 11, 9:30 AM. Fifteen Old Testament readings, the transformation of the vestments from dark to white, and the proclamation that the day of Resurrection has come.

Pascha begins at 11:00 PM on Saturday, April 11. We gather outside in the dark, process around the church three times with candles, then enter proclaiming the Resurrection. The Paschal Divine Liturgy follows. Come. Bring a candle. Stay as long as you are able.

All of this together is Holy Week. You do not need to understand everything before you arrive. You need to arrive. The services will do the rest.

Fr. Solomon
articles.author: Fr. Solomon Longo
articles.title: Holy Week 2026: A Complete Guide to Every Service at Sts. Peter and Paul

articles.excerpt: St. Vladimir's Online School of Theology is offering a Certified Catechist training course from April 20 to July 30, with a registration deadline of April 13th.
articles.body: St. Vladimir's Online School of Theology is offering a Certified Catechist training course with the blessings of Metropolitan Tikhon and Metropolitan Saba. The course runs April 20 to July 30, with a registration deadline of April 13th. Instructors include both Metropolitans, Archimandrite Jeremy Davis, and Fr. George Parsenios. With the number of catechumens growing across North America, trained catechists are needed. If you are called to teach the faith, this course is worth serious consideration.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Certified Catechist Training Course at SVS: Registration Deadline April 13th

articles.excerpt: Father Solomon will attend the Theological Breakfast hosted by the Coalition of Latino Pastors and Ministers of New Jersey, representing Sts. Peter and Paul and the Orthodox Christian tradition.
articles.body: This Saturday, Father Solomon will attend the Theological Breakfast hosted by the Coalition of Latino Pastors and Ministers of New Jersey, representing Sts. Peter and Paul and the Orthodox Christian tradition. The breakfast brings together clergy and ministers from across denominations for theological conversation over a shared meal, the kind of gathering where real dialogue tends to happen more easily than it does at formal events. Father Solomon's participation reflects the Cathedral's ongoing relationship with the broader Christian community serving New Jersey's Latino population. If you have questions or thoughts you would like him to bring to the conversation, reach out before Saturday.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Theological Breakfast with the Coalition of Latino Pastors and Ministers

articles.excerpt: HPHA and Jersey City Council members Frank Gilmore and Eleana Little are hosting a neighborhood clean-up throughout Paulus Hook this Saturday.
articles.body: This Saturday, HPHA and Jersey City Council members Frank Gilmore (Ward F) and Eleana Little (Ward E) are hosting a neighborhood clean-up throughout Paulus Hook. Parishioners are encouraged to come out and join their neighbors. The Cathedral is part of this neighborhood, and showing up for events like this is one of the more straightforward ways we demonstrate that.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Neighborhood Clean-Up with Council Members Gilmore and Little

articles.excerpt: The Historic Paulus Hook Association is organizing an Earth Day clean-up of Peninsula Park on Wednesday from 1 to 4 PM. The Cathedral is supplying bags, brooms, and gloves.
articles.body: This Wednesday from 1 to 4 PM, the Historic Paulus Hook Association is organizing an Earth Day clean-up of Peninsula Park (Morris Canal Park). The Cathedral is supplying bags, brooms, and gloves. Meet on Washington Street near the Korean War Memorial to get your supplies before heading into the park. Three hours, good work, close to home. Come if you can.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Earth Day Clean-Up at Peninsula Park with HPHA

articles.excerpt: Two events on Sunday evening: the Cathedral participates in Reves de Renouveau (Dreams of Renewal), and parishioners are invited to a screening of El Tonto por Cristo, a Spanish-language film on the fool-for-Christ tradition.
articles.body: Two things happening Sunday evening, April 19th. The Cathedral is participating in Reves de Renouveau (Dreams of Renewal), a special cultural and spiritual event. On the same evening, parishioners are invited to a screening of El Tonto por Cristo (The Fool for Christ), a Spanish-language film exploring the ancient tradition of the fool for Christ, saints who embraced radical humility and apparent foolishness as a way of living the Gospel without compromise. It is a tradition that is harder to explain than to encounter. Both events are worth your time.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Reves de Renouveau and Screening of El Tonto por Cristo

articles.excerpt: His Eminence Archbishop Michael is presiding over the annual Pan-Orthodox Vespers in Perth Amboy for the Sunday of Orthodoxy, and the Cathedral community is encouraged to attend and bring their icons.
articles.body: This Sunday is the Sunday of Orthodoxy, the first Sunday of Great Lent and the day the Church commemorates the restoration of icon veneration at the Seventh Ecumenical Council in 787 AD. His Eminence Archbishop Michael is presiding over the annual Pan-Orthodox Vespers in Perth Amboy, and the Cathedral community is encouraged to attend. Orthodox Christians from parishes across the Diocese of New York and New Jersey will be there, carrying their parish icons in procession. It is one of the few occasions each year when the whole diocese prays together in one place. Come and bring your icons.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Sunday of Orthodoxy Vespers with Archbishop Michael in Perth Amboy

articles.excerpt: The Feast of the Annunciation falls this year on Tuesday, March 25th. Great Vespers is Monday evening, March 24th, with Divine Liturgy at 9:00 AM on Tuesday morning. Fish is permitted today.
articles.body: This Tuesday, March 25th, is the Feast of the Annunciation, nine months before Christmas and one of the Twelve Great Feasts of the Church. Great Vespers will be served Monday evening, March 24th, with Divine Liturgy at 9:00 AM on Tuesday morning. The feast falls in the middle of Great Lent this year, but the fasting rules are relaxed: fish is permitted today. The church will be open 1 to 5 PM following Liturgy, with Catechism class at 8 PM. Mary's "yes" to the Archangel is what made everything else possible. Come and celebrate it.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Annunciation: Great Vespers and Divine Liturgy

articles.excerpt: The Great Canon of St. Andrew of Crete will be read in two parts: Monday, February 24th and Wednesday, February 26th. These are among the most important services of the first week of Lent.
articles.body: The Great Canon of St. Andrew of Crete will be read in two parts this week: Monday, February 24th and Wednesday, February 26th. Written in the 8th century, the Canon consists of 250 stanzas moving through the Old and New Testaments, holding each biblical figure up against our own lives and failures. The refrain is always the same: "Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me." Prostrations accompany the service. If you are unable to make full prostrations for physical reasons, remain kneeling and prostrate from that position when you can. These are among the most important services of the first week of Lent. Do not miss them if you can help it.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: The Great Canon of St. Andrew of Crete

articles.excerpt: This Sunday evening is Forgiveness Vespers, the service that begins Great Lent. At the end, the entire community will approach one another to ask and receive forgiveness for all offenses of the past year.
articles.body: This Sunday evening is Forgiveness Vespers, the service that begins Great Lent. It starts with the bright, festive tone of a Sunday Vespers and shifts midway into the first somber notes of the Lenten season. At the end, the entire community will approach one another, one by one, to ask and receive forgiveness for all offenses of the past year, known and unknown. Many people find this the most difficult and most necessary service of the year. Great Lent begins tonight. Come ready for it.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Forgiveness Vespers: Great Lent Begins This Evening

articles.excerpt: Holy Resurrection Orthodox Church in Wayne is hosting a Mardi Gras Celebration this Saturday evening as a fundraiser, open to Orthodox young adults in their 20s and 30s from across the diocese.
articles.body: Holy Resurrection Orthodox Church in Wayne is hosting a Mardi Gras Celebration this Saturday evening as a fundraiser for capital improvements to their building. This is an Orthodox Young Adults event, open to parishioners in their 20s and 30s from across the diocese. The timing is right: Mardi Gras falls before Lent begins, and there is no better reason for a celebration than what comes after it. Come out, support a sister parish, and meet Orthodox young adults from around the region. Details and tickets are linked below.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Mardi Gras Celebration at Holy Resurrection Church in Wayne

articles.excerpt: The 'R' Club and FOCA are hosting the 2026 Gifts of Love fundraiser in support of the St. Olga Church Fund for Orthodox mission work in Alaska. Snow date February 8th if needed.
articles.body: The R Club and FOCA are hosting the 2026 Gifts of Love fundraiser this Sunday, February 1st, in support of the St. Olga Church Fund for Orthodox mission work in Alaska. If the weather does not cooperate, the snow date is February 8th. Alaska's Orthodox communities are among the oldest on the continent and among the most financially stretched. This is one of the ways we stay connected to that work. Please give generously.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: FOCA Gifts of Love Fundraiser in Support of Orthodox Mission Work in Alaska

articles.excerpt: Great Vespers is Saturday evening, February 1st. Divine Liturgy is Sunday morning, February 2nd. Candles will be blessed at the Liturgy. Bring candles to take home.
articles.body: This weekend the Cathedral celebrates the Meeting of Our Lord in the Temple, forty days after the Nativity. Great Vespers is Saturday evening, February 1st. Divine Liturgy is Sunday morning, February 2nd. Candles will be blessed at the Liturgy. Bring candles to be blessed and take them home; they are used throughout the year at times of prayer, illness, and difficulty. The feast commemorates the righteous Simeon, who had been promised he would not die before seeing the Messiah, taking the infant Christ in his arms in the Temple and singing what became the Church's evening prayer: "Lord, now let your servant depart in peace." After decades of waiting, he saw what he was promised. Plan to be there.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Meeting of Our Lord in the Temple

articles.excerpt: Nick Rus is leading a working team to explore whether the Cathedral can establish a parish preschool or daycare. Consultants have visited the hall and Archbishop Michael has expressed support.
articles.body: Nick Rus is leading a working team to explore whether the Cathedral can establish a parish preschool or daycare. Consultants from Hillmann Consulting have visited the hall to assess the space. Archbishop Michael has expressed support for the initiative. The team is currently researching licensing requirements, costs, and potential revenue. If you have relevant experience or want to be involved, reach out to Nick or Father Solomon.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Parish Preschool and Daycare Exploration Underway

articles.excerpt: The Annual Parish Assembly will be held this Sunday, January 18th during coffee hour. Father Solomon will present the 2026 Budget Draft and review the past year's activities.
articles.body: The Annual Parish Assembly will be held this Sunday, January 18th during coffee hour. Father Solomon will present the 2026 Budget Draft and review the past year's activities. This is also the moment to raise questions about parish direction, voice concerns, and hear what is being planned, including the ongoing exploration of a parish preschool, upcoming building needs, and ministry goals for the year ahead. Parish governance in the OCA belongs to the laity as much as to the clergy. Please come and participate.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Annual Parish Assembly: Budget, Plans, and Parish Direction for 2026

articles.body: Following Theophany, Father Solomon is scheduling house blessings throughout January and February. The sign-up sheet is at the candle stand. He will visit your home, pray in each room, and sprinkle the walls with Theophany water. Please sign up so he can plan his schedule by neighborhood.

articles.body: This Saturday at 11 AM, the parish is hosting a baby shower for Tamar and Julie Wright as they prepare to welcome their new child. Please come and celebrate with them. If you would like to bring a gift, information about what the family needs is available from the coordinators. The Wrights are part of this community, and this is what community does.

articles.excerpt: This Saturday at noon, the Cathedral will celebrate the Holy Baptism of Tamara. All are welcome to attend and welcome Tamara into the life of the Church.
articles.body: This Saturday at noon, the Cathedral will celebrate the Holy Baptism of Tamara. Two choir members will sing during the service, coordinated by Gleb Ivanov. The baptism will include triple immersion, chrismation, the white baptismal garment, and tonsuring of hair in the form of a cross. Tamara's godmother will make the baptismal vows and commit to her spiritual formation going forward. All are welcome to attend and welcome Tamara into the life of the Church. The baby shower begins at 11 AM, so plan to stay for both.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Holy Baptism of Tamara

articles.excerpt: Beginning January 7th, Wednesday evening Catechism Classes meet at 8 PM through April 1st, replacing the regular Bible Study. Open to inquirers, catechumens, and any parishioner.
articles.body: Beginning January 7th, Wednesday evening Catechism Classes are replacing the regular Bible Study through April 1st, meeting at 8 PM. The curriculum covers the Holy Trinity, the Creed, the sacraments, prayer, fasting, and Orthodox moral teaching. Open to inquirers, catechumens, and any parishioner who wants a more grounded understanding of the faith. Same time slot as Bible Study; same Zoom link.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Wednesday Catechism Classes Begin January 7th Through April

articles.excerpt: The Feast of Theophany is this week. Great Vespers is Sunday evening, January 5th at 7:00 PM. Divine Liturgy is Monday morning, January 6th at 9:00 AM. Bring bottles for blessed water.
articles.body: The Feast of Theophany is this week. Great Vespers is Sunday evening, January 5th at 7:00 PM. Divine Liturgy is Monday morning, January 6th at 9:00 AM. The Great Blessing of Waters will be performed at both services. Please bring bottles to take blessed water home. Theophany water is kept throughout the year and used for blessing homes, in times of illness, and in daily prayer. Following the feast, Father Solomon will begin scheduling house blessings; watch for the sign-up. Theophany reminds us that at Christ's Baptism, the Holy Trinity was revealed fully for the first time: the Son in the water, the Spirit as a dove, the Father's voice from heaven. Come and celebrate it.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Theophany: The Great Blessing of Waters

articles.excerpt: Father Solomon and Matushka Anna are hosting a Christmastide party at the rectory this Friday, January 2nd at 5:00 PM. Please RSVP by email.
articles.body: Father Solomon and Matushka Anna are hosting a Christmastide party at the rectory this Friday, January 2nd at 5:00 PM. The Nativity season runs through Theophany on January 6th, so we are still in it. Please RSVP by email so they can plan accordingly. Come and extend the celebration a little further.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Christmastide Party at the Rectory

articles.excerpt: His Eminence Archbishop Michael is with us this weekend. During the Sunday Liturgy he will tonsure Ero Ogbevire to the minor clerical orders of Reader and Subdeacon.
articles.body: His Eminence Archbishop Michael is with us this weekend. Vespers is Saturday evening. Divine Liturgy is Sunday morning at 9:00 AM (note the earlier start time). During the Liturgy, the Archbishop will tonsure our own Ero Ogbevire to the minor clerical orders of Reader and Subdeacon. Ero will be formally installed to read the Epistle and appointed readings at services, and to assist at the altar. Please arrive before the Archbishop's entrance. A potluck coffee hour follows: please bring something substantial to share. This is a weekend worth clearing your schedule for.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Archbishop Michael Visits: Tonsure of Ero Ogbevire to Reader and Subdeacon

articles.excerpt: The annual Christmas Gala is Monday, December 22nd at 7:30 PM. Archbishop Michael will be in attendance. Tickets on Eventbrite; use code SSPPNJ50 for 50% off as a parishioner.
articles.body: The annual Christmas Gala is this Monday, December 22nd at 7:30 PM. Archbishop Michael will be in attendance following his weekend visit. Tickets are available on Eventbrite; use code SSPPNJ50 for 50% off as a parishioner. The evening includes a concert of Orthodox sacred music and Christmas carols performed by professional musicians in the Cathedral nave, followed by a reception with food. Proceeds support church preservation. If you have been meaning to bring a friend or neighbor to something at the Cathedral, this is the event to bring them to.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Christmas Gala 2025: Sacred Music and Reception in the Cathedral Nave

articles.excerpt: Before the Gala on December 22nd at 3:30 PM, the Cathedral hosts a conversation between Fr. Solomon Longo and Fr. Nicholas Fine about the real experience of priestly ministry after ordination.
articles.body: Before the Gala on Monday, December 22nd at 3:30 PM, the Cathedral is hosting "Good Work Begun: Discussing First Years in Ministry," a conversation between Fr. Solomon Longo (St. Tikhon's Seminary, OCA) and Fr. Nicholas Fine (St. Vladimir's Seminary, Antiochian Archdiocese) about the real experience of priestly ministry in the years after ordination. This is designed for seminarians, those discerning a vocation, and clergy who want an honest conversation about the gap between seminary and parish life. Heavy hors d'oeuvres will be served at 3:30. Seminary faculty and students attend free, courtesy of a generous parishioner and SVS trustee. Arrive early to beat rush hour.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Good Work Begun: A Conversation on First Years in Ministry

articles.excerpt: Great Compline is Christmas Eve, December 24th at 6:30 PM. Divine Liturgy is Christmas morning, December 25th at 9:30 AM. Christ is born. Come and glorify Him.
articles.body: Great Compline is Christmas Eve, December 24th at 6:30 PM. Divine Liturgy is Christmas morning, December 25th at 9:30 AM. Parking is available across the street for both services. Archbishop Michael's Nativity pastoral letter has been distributed and is available at the candle stand. There is no fasting on Wednesdays and Fridays through the twelve days of Christmas. Christ is born. Come and glorify Him.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Nativity of Our Lord: Christmas Eve and Christmas Morning Services

articles.excerpt: Volunteers are needed for a Food Giveaway and Service in Newark on Christmas Day. If you are willing to give part of your Christmas morning to serve those with very little, please sign up.
articles.body: We are looking for parishioners willing to volunteer on Christmas Day for a Food Giveaway and Service in Newark. This is not a small ask: it means giving up part of your Christmas morning to serve people who have very little of what the holiday is supposed to look like. If you are willing to do that, please sign up using the information below. "Whatever you did for one of the least of these, you did for me."
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Christmas Day Food Giveaway in Newark: Volunteers Needed

articles.excerpt: The Feast of St. Nicholas is this weekend. Vespers is Friday, December 5th at 6:30 PM. Divine Liturgy is Saturday, December 6th at 9:00 AM, with a special coffee hour following on Sunday.
articles.body: The Feast of St. Nicholas is this weekend. Vespers is Friday, December 5th at 6:30 PM. Divine Liturgy is Saturday, December 6th at 9:00 AM. A special celebratory coffee hour will follow on Sunday, December 7th, with the Nativity Season Children's Choir singing a St. Nicholas song they have been learning. A special guest may appear. If you can add something to the coffee hour table on Sunday to supplement the usual spread, please do. St. Nicholas, Bishop of Myra, defender of the faith at Nicaea and friend of the poor: his feast deserves a proper celebration.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of St. Nicholas: Vespers, Liturgy, and a Special Coffee Hour

articles.excerpt: All children aged 2 and older are welcome to join the Nativity Season Children's Choir. Rehearsals happen after the veneration of the Cross during Sunday Liturgy, while adults are still venerating.
articles.body: All children aged 2 and older are welcome to join the Nativity Season Children's Choir. Rehearsals happen after the veneration of the Cross during Sunday Liturgy, while adults are still venerating. The choir will sing at the St. Nicholas coffee hour and at Christmas services. No experience needed. Just bring the children and let them learn. Gleb Ivanov is coordinating with Father Solomon on the repertoire.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Nativity Season Children's Choir: All Children 2 and Older Welcome

articles.excerpt: This Sunday the Cathedral is hosting the Distinguished Diocesan Benefactors Dinner in the parish hall. Archbishop Michael will preside.
articles.body: This Sunday the Cathedral is hosting the Distinguished Diocesan Benefactors Dinner in the parish hall. Archbishop Michael will preside. The dinner recognizes the major donors whose giving supports St. Andrew's Camp, newly-ordained priests, mission parishes, and the broader work of the Diocese of New York and New Jersey. Hosting this dinner here is a responsibility the Cathedral takes seriously, and parishioners will have the chance to meet and speak with some of the people whose generosity makes diocesan ministry possible. More details are available from Father Solomon.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Distinguished Diocesan Benefactors Dinner Hosted at the Cathedral

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral celebrates the marriage of Eugene Ioannou and Lauren Freedman this Saturday, November 8th at 2:00 PM. Please keep them in your prayers.
articles.body: The Cathedral celebrates the marriage of Eugene Ioannou and Lauren Freedman this Saturday, November 8th at 2:00 PM. A rehearsal will be held Friday, November 7th at 3:30 PM. Eugene and Lauren completed five Pre-Cana sessions with Father Solomon and coordinated with Lauren's Greek Orthodox home parish for her letter of good standing. The service includes the traditional Byzantine rite of crowning, the shared cup, and the triple procession around the ceremonial table. Archbishop Michael, familiar with Greek wedding customs, has been consulted. Please keep Eugene and Lauren in your prayers as they prepare for this sacrament.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Wedding of Eugene Ioannou and Lauren Freedman

articles.body: The Cathedral celebrates the marriage of the Bruining and Daringa families this Sunday, November 9th. articles.body: This Saturday at 9 AM, the Cathedral celebrates the reception of five people into the Orthodox Church. Jake Brown, Nory Jean, Daryle Jean Louis, and Matt Welty will be baptized. Caroline Chiorean, previously baptized in another Christian tradition, will be received through Chrismation. articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Mountain Men's Respite Retreat: September 19-21

articles.excerpt: The Northern New Jersey IPA Synergy Group holds its kick-off this Tuesday at St. Anthony Orthodox Church in Bergenfield, bringing together Orthodox faithful from multiple parishes for shared ministry.
articles.body: The Northern New Jersey IPA (International Partnership of Apostolates) Synergy Group is holding its kick-off this Tuesday, September 16th at St. Anthony Orthodox Church in Bergenfield. The group is bringing together Orthodox faithful from multiple parishes to coordinate shared ministry and avoid duplicating effort across the region. Interested parishioners are welcome to attend.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Northern NJ IPA Synergy Group Kick-off at St. Anthony's in Bergenfield

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral is offering the sacrament of Holy Unction this Saturday at 9:30 AM. Holy Unction is not reserved for the gravely ill; it is for anyone seeking God's healing of body and soul.
articles.body: The Cathedral is offering the sacrament of Holy Unction this Saturday, September 13th at 9:30 AM. Holy Unction is not reserved for the gravely ill; it is for anyone seeking God's healing of body and soul. The service includes seven epistle and gospel readings and seven anointings with blessed oil. Plan to be there for the full service if you are able.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Holy Unction at the Cathedral: Saturday, September 13th

articles.excerpt: Great Vespers for the Nativity of the Theotokos is Sunday evening, and Divine Liturgy is Monday morning, September 8th at 9 AM. The feast opens the liturgical year's cycle of major celebrations.
articles.body: This Sunday evening is Great Vespers for the Nativity of the Theotokos, and Divine Liturgy is Monday morning, September 8th at 9 AM. The feast celebrates the birth of the Virgin Mary to Joachim and Anna and opens the liturgical new year's cycle of major celebrations. Monday is a weekday; come if your schedule allows.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Nativity of the Theotokos

articles.excerpt: Starting this Saturday, September 6th, the Cathedral is launching a monthly Saturday Night gathering for teens and kids. Dinner is included. The goal is a regular place for young people to gather in an Orthodox context.
articles.body: Starting this Saturday, September 6th, the Cathedral is launching a monthly Saturday Night gathering for teens and kids. Dinner is included; meet after pizza. The goal is straightforward: a regular place for young people to gather with others their age in an Orthodox context. It will meet monthly going forward. Speak to Father Solomon if you have questions or want to help.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Monthly Saturday Night Gathering for Teens and Kids Launches

articles.excerpt: Church Clean-Up Day is this Saturday, August 23rd. We will be deep-cleaning the nave and altar area, polishing fixtures, organizing storage, and working on the grounds. Tasks for all ages and abilities.
articles.body: Church Clean-Up Day is this Saturday, August 23rd. We will be deep-cleaning the nave and altar area, polishing fixtures, organizing storage, and working on the grounds. Tasks for all ages and abilities. Coffee and snacks provided. Come for as long as you can.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Church Clean-Up Day: Saturday, August 23rd

articles.excerpt: The Orthodox Young Adults are hosting a Movie Night this Saturday evening, August 23rd. Relaxed, informal, and open to young adults from any parish.
articles.body: The Orthodox Young Adults are hosting a Movie Night this Saturday evening, August 23rd. Relaxed, informal, and open to young adults from any parish. Details on location and film are linked below.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Orthodox Young Adults Movie Night

articles.excerpt: Great Vespers for the Transfiguration is Tuesday evening, August 5th at 7 PM. Divine Liturgy is Wednesday morning, August 6th at 9 AM. Bring grapes, apples, or other summer fruit for the traditional blessing.
articles.body: Great Vespers for the Transfiguration is Tuesday evening, August 5th at 7 PM. Divine Liturgy is Wednesday morning, August 6th at 9 AM. Bring grapes, apples, or other summer fruit for the traditional blessing of first fruits at the end of the Liturgy. Father will explain the Transfiguration icon to the children after the service. The blessed fruit will be shared. Come to both services if you can.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Transfiguration: Bring Fruit for the Blessing

articles.excerpt: The diocese is organizing two volunteer days to help St. John the Baptist Church in Spring Valley, NY with building repairs: Saturday, August 2nd and Saturday, August 9th.
articles.body: The diocese is organizing two volunteer days to help St. John the Baptist Church in Spring Valley, NY with building repairs: Saturday, August 2nd and Saturday, August 9th. Show up with work clothes and whatever tools you have. The Cathedral is sending people on both Saturdays. Sign up so we can coordinate transportation.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Diocese Volunteer Days at St. John the Baptist Church in Spring Valley

articles.excerpt: The annual parish picnic is this Saturday, July 26th. Bring a dish to share, come hungry, and plan to stay a while.
articles.body: The annual parish picnic is this Saturday, July 26th. Bring a dish to share, come hungry, and plan to stay a while. Food, outdoor games, and the whole Cathedral community in one place. These afternoons are better than they sound on paper.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Annual Parish Picnic: Saturday, July 26th

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral has received small relics of several saints, now available for veneration. Father Solomon will announce the identities of the saints and their placement in the church on Sunday.
articles.body: The Cathedral has received small relics of several saints, now available for veneration. Father Solomon will announce the identities of the saints and their placement in the church on Sunday. The relics will be available for veneration after Liturgy.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Cathedral Receives Small Relics of Several Saints

articles.excerpt: All three sets of the Cathedral's front doors have been refinished this summer and the main door closing mechanism has been replaced. Good stewardship of a historic building means attending to these things.
articles.body: All three sets of the Cathedral's front doors have been refinished this summer and the main door closing mechanism has been replaced. The doors now close properly and look as they should. Good stewardship of a historic building means attending to these things before they become serious problems.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Cathedral Front Doors Refinished This Summer

articles.excerpt: This Sunday evening at 6 PM, the Cathedral hosts a concert centered on Mussorgsky's Pictures at an Exhibition, alongside works by Mendelssohn, Faure, and Schubert. Use code SSPPNJ50 for 50% off.
articles.body: This Sunday evening at 6 PM, the Cathedral hosts "Summer Serenade: An Evening of Stillness and Fire with the Pianoforte." The program centers on Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition," alongside works by Mendelssohn, Faure, and Schubert. Tickets are on Eventbrite; use code SSPPNJ50 for 50% off. A reception with food follows the concert. Proceeds support Cathedral preservation. Bring someone who has never been inside this building before.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Summer Serenade: An Evening of Stillness and Fire with the Pianoforte

articles.excerpt: This Sunday before Divine Liturgy, Gabriel Torres will be received as a catechumen of the Orthodox Church, formally enrolling him among those preparing for baptism.
articles.body: This Sunday before Divine Liturgy, Gabriel Torres will be received as a catechumen of the Orthodox Church. The rite formally enrolls him among those preparing for baptism. The whole parish is invited to witness and to pray for Gabriel as he begins this part of his journey.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Making of a Catechumen: Gabriel Torres

articles.excerpt: This Sunday is Pentecost, with Vespers following Liturgy including the Kneeling Prayers of St. Basil the Great: the first time since Pascha that Orthodox Christians kneel in prayer. Plan to stay.
articles.body: This Sunday is Pentecost. Divine Liturgy in the morning, with the church in green. The Vespers of Pentecost will be served following Liturgy, including the Kneeling Prayers of St. Basil the Great: three long prayers during which the congregation kneels together for the first time since Pascha. As Father Solomon noted, it is the first time in nearly two months that Orthodox Christians kneel in prayer. Plan to stay for Vespers.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Pentecost: Divine Liturgy and the Kneeling Prayers of St. Basil the Great

articles.excerpt: A priest friend performed an emergency baptism for premature twin boys, Peter and Paul, in the NICU where they were fighting for their lives. Both boys survived. Please keep them and their family in your prayers.
articles.body: A priest friend performed an emergency baptism for premature twin boys, Peter and Paul, in the NICU where they were fighting for their lives. Both boys survived. The names Peter and Paul connect these children to our Cathedral's own patron saints. Please keep them and their family in your prayers.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Emergency Baptism of Twin Boys Peter and Paul in the NICU

articles.excerpt: This Thursday, May 8th, a hierarchical Divine Liturgy will be celebrated at the Cathedral marking the 15th Anniversary of Archbishop Michael's consecration to the episcopate. All are welcome.
articles.body: This Thursday, May 8th, a hierarchical Divine Liturgy will be celebrated at the Cathedral marking the 15th Anniversary of Archbishop Michael's consecration to the episcopate. Priests from across the diocese will concelebrate. Parking information has been shared with clergy RSVPs. A festive lunch and reception follow the Liturgy. All are welcome to attend and honor the Archbishop's fifteen years of service.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: 15th Anniversary of Archbishop Michael's Consecration: Hierarchical Divine Liturgy

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral is gathering this Saturday, May 10th at 10 AM at the north side playground of Liberty State Park. Bring snacks, a dish to share, blankets, and whatever you need for a morning outside.
articles.body: The Cathedral is gathering this Saturday, May 10th at 10 AM at the north side playground of Liberty State Park for a spring picnic. Bring snacks, a dish to share, blankets, and whatever you need for a morning outside. There is a hint of rain in the forecast but it looks light and should clear. Come anyway.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Spring Picnic at Liberty State Park

articles.excerpt: The annual Memorial Day Pilgrimage to St. Tikhon's Monastery in South Canaan, Pennsylvania is Memorial Day weekend. The pilgrimage has run for over a century. Carpools are being organized.
articles.body: The annual Memorial Day Pilgrimage to St. Tikhon's Monastery in South Canaan, Pennsylvania is Memorial Day weekend. The pilgrimage has run for over a century and draws Orthodox Christians from across the eastern seaboard. The weekend includes hierarchical Divine Liturgies, prayer at the graves of departed hierarchs, and opportunities for confession. Carpools are being organized. Sign up if you plan to go.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Annual Memorial Day Pilgrimage to St. Tikhon's Monastery

articles.excerpt: On May 2nd, the Cathedral hosted Lumiere d'Harmonie, an evening of French classical repertoire. French classical music in an Orthodox cathedral is an unusual pairing, and that is part of what made the evening interesting.
articles.body: On May 2nd, the Cathedral hosted "Lumiere d'Harmonie: An Evening with the French Masters of Classical Music." French classical repertoire in an Orthodox cathedral is an unusual pairing, and that is part of what made the evening interesting. The Cathedral has been building a reputation as a venue for serious music, and this concert added another chapter to that effort.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Lumiere d'Harmonie: An Evening with the French Masters of Classical Music

articles.excerpt: Metropolitan Tikhon and Archbishop Michael participated in the glorification of Matushka Olga of Alaska, formally declaring her a saint of the Church. Icons are being distributed to parishes across the OCA.
articles.body: Metropolitan Tikhon and Archbishop Michael have participated in the glorification of Matushka Olga of Alaska, formally declaring her a saint of the Church. Icons of the newly-canonized St. Olga are being distributed to parishes across the OCA. Her feast day has been established. She served faithfully in Alaska's mission field and is now among our intercessors. Memory eternal, and welcome to the calendar.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Glorification of Matushka Olga of Alaska: A New Saint for the Church

articles.excerpt: On April 26th, Father Solomon traveled to Graceland and Bayview Cemeteries to bless the graves of parishioners who have fallen asleep in the Lord.
articles.body: On April 26th, Father Solomon traveled to Graceland and Bayview Cemeteries to bless the graves of parishioners who have fallen asleep in the Lord. Cemetery grave blessings are one of the quieter pastoral duties of an Orthodox priest, and one of the more meaningful ones. Standing at a grave and praying for someone by name is a concrete act of the Church's belief that death is not the end of the story.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Grave Blessings at Graceland and Bayview Cemeteries

articles.excerpt: The parish gathered at Liberty State Park on April 5th for a spring picnic. Liberty State Park is a short distance from the Cathedral, and the views are a good reason to use it.
articles.body: The parish gathered at Liberty State Park on April 5th for a spring picnic with an exceptional backdrop: the Statue of Liberty and the Manhattan skyline visible across the water. Liberty State Park is a short distance from the Cathedral, and using it as the picnic site gave the day a particular Jersey City quality that a backyard or church hall cannot replicate.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Spring Picnic at Liberty State Park with the Statue of Liberty in View

articles.excerpt: Orthodox women from the Cathedral and neighboring parishes gathered at St. Andrew's Camp from March 28th to 30th for a Lenten retreat focused on prayer and quiet.
articles.body: Orthodox women from the Cathedral and neighboring parishes gathered at St. Andrew's Camp from March 28th to 30th for a Lenten retreat. A weekend away during the Great Fast, focused on prayer and quiet, is a different experience than the daily rhythm of Lenten services at home. Those who attended came back noticeably quieter in a good way.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Women's Lenten Retreat at St. Andrew's Camp

articles.excerpt: The Historic Paulus Hook Association held one of its regular meetings at the Cathedral in March, with Jersey City's Forester as a guest speaker discussing urban forestry and tree preservation.
articles.body: The Historic Paulus Hook Association held one of its regular meetings at the Cathedral in March, with Jersey City's Forester as a guest speaker. The discussion focused on urban forestry and tree preservation in the Paulus Hook neighborhood. The Cathedral's role as a host for HPHA meetings reflects its position as an anchor institution in the neighborhood, not just a place of worship.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: HPHA Meeting at the Cathedral with Jersey City Forester as Guest

articles.excerpt: In March 2025, the parish received news of the repose of Fr. Dan Skvir. His funeral services were held at St. Vladimir Orthodox Church in Trenton. Memory eternal.
articles.body: In March 2025, the parish received news of the repose of Fr. Dan Skvir. His funeral services were held at St. Vladimir Orthodox Church in Trenton, and parishioners were invited to attend. The death of a priest is always felt across parish lines in a diocese as interconnected as New York and New Jersey, and Fr. Dan's passing was mourned widely. Memory eternal.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Repose of Fr. Dan Skvir

articles.excerpt: Vendors from the Mount of Olives Treasury came to the Cathedral in March, bringing handcrafted items carved by Christian artisans in Jerusalem and Bethlehem.
articles.body: Vendors from the Mount of Olives Treasury came to the Cathedral in March, bringing handcrafted items carved by Christian artisans in Jerusalem and Bethlehem. Purchasing from this ministry supports the Christians who live and work in the Holy Land, a community that depends in part on exactly this kind of direct connection with parishes abroad.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Mount of Olives Treasury Vendors Visit the Cathedral

articles.excerpt: The 40th day after a death is one of the most significant memorial moments in Orthodox tradition. In March the parish observed the 40th day following the repose of Olga Lometz with a Panikhida at the Cathedral and at Greenville Memorial Home.
articles.body: The 40th day after a death is one of the most significant memorial moments in Orthodox tradition, and in March the parish observed the 40th day following the repose of Olga Lometz with a Panikhida at the Cathedral and at Greenville Memorial Home. Gathering at the place where someone died or is being remembered, as well as in the church, is a pastoral commitment to the family and to the memory of the departed. Memory eternal.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Panikhida for Olga Lometz on the 40th Day

articles.excerpt: This Sunday is the Sunday of Orthodoxy, and a Pan-Orthodox Vespers is being held in the area, bringing together Orthodox Christians from Greek, Antiochian, OCA, and other parishes for a shared service.
articles.body: This Sunday is the Sunday of Orthodoxy, and a Pan-Orthodox Vespers is being held in the area, bringing together Orthodox Christians from Greek, Antiochian, OCA, and other parishes for a shared service. The celebration commemorates the 843 AD restoration of icons to the Church. Parishioners are encouraged to bring their parish icons for the procession. Location and time are listed below.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Sunday of Orthodoxy: Pan-Orthodox Vespers

articles.excerpt: St. Vladimir's Seminary hosted a book launch on February 20th featuring Dr. Razvan Porumb, including theological discussion and a Q&A. The event was promoted to Cathedral parishioners.
articles.body: St. Vladimir's Seminary hosted a book launch on February 20th featuring Dr. Razvan Porumb, and the event was promoted to Cathedral parishioners. The launch included theological discussion and a Q&A, the kind of academic-adjacent event that the Cathedral community has a taste for. St. Vladimir's is close enough to Jersey City that events there function almost as an extension of parish life for those who make the trip.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Book Launch at St. Vladimir's Seminary: Dr. Razvan Porumb

articles.excerpt: St. Nicholas Antiochian Orthodox Church hosted a Pan-Orthodox Faith and Family Night on February 15th that drew over 15 churches from multiple jurisdictions. His Grace Bishop JOHN Abdalah attended.
articles.body: St. Nicholas Antiochian Orthodox Church hosted a Pan-Orthodox Faith and Family Night on February 15th that drew over 15 churches from multiple jurisdictions. His Grace Bishop JOHN Abdalah attended, and Father Peter Orfanakos served as guest speaker. Events of this scale, pulling together Antiochian, OCA, Greek, and other Orthodox parishes in one room, are relatively rare and reflect growing cooperation among the Orthodox jurisdictions in the New York-New Jersey area.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Pan-Orthodox Faith and Family Night with Bishop JOHN Abdalah

articles.excerpt: A FOCA Gifts of Love fundraiser during coffee hour in February raised funds for St. Theodosius Cathedral, which had suffered fire damage and needed help with hall repairs.
articles.body: A FOCA Gifts of Love fundraiser during coffee hour in February raised funds for St. Theodosius Cathedral, which had suffered fire damage and needed help with hall repairs. Inter-parish solidarity is a real thing in the OCA, and when a cathedral community is struggling, other parishes show up. Sts. Peter and Paul showed up.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: FOCA Gifts of Love Fundraiser for St. Theodosius Cathedral After Fire Damage

articles.excerpt: His Eminence Archbishop Michael came to the Cathedral for Vespers and Divine Liturgy in February, and stayed for a Q&A session during coffee hour, giving parishioners the chance to put questions directly to the Archbishop.
articles.body: His Eminence Archbishop Michael came to the Cathedral for Vespers and Divine Liturgy in February, and stayed for a Q&A session during coffee hour. Parishioners had the chance to put questions directly to the Archbishop about the faith, the diocese, and Orthodox life in New Jersey. That kind of open conversation between an archbishop and a parish community is not always available, and the turnout reflected how much people valued it.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Archbishop Michael Visits for Vespers, Liturgy, and an Open Q&A

articles.excerpt: Prior to Vespers in February, a Panikhida was celebrated for the repose of Tatiana Baeva. Memory eternal.
articles.body: Prior to Vespers in February, a Panikhida was celebrated for the repose of Tatiana Baeva. Memorial services woven into the regular schedule of Vespers are one of the ways the parish maintains its practice of praying for the dead not just at funerals but as an ongoing part of its life together. Memory eternal.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Panikhida for the Repose of Tatiana Baeva

articles.excerpt: On January 19th, the Cathedral celebrated the reception of the Orsi family, Vanessa Lane, Aidan Willis, and Ambree McAhee into the Orthodox Church, while Dylan Wells was received as a catechumen.
articles.body: On January 19th, the Cathedral celebrated one of its most significant days in recent memory: the Orsi family, Vanessa Lane, Aidan Willis, and Ambree McAhee were baptized and/or chrismated, while Dylan Wells was received as a catechumen. Multiple baptisms and chrismations in a single service are a sign of a parish that is genuinely growing, and January 19th was that kind of morning.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: A Significant Day: Multiple Baptisms and Chrismations on January 19th

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral launched its annual census in January 2025 to update membership records and contact information. A census is administrative work, but it has real pastoral implications.
articles.body: The Cathedral launched its annual census in January 2025 to update membership records and contact information. A census is administrative work, but it has pastoral implications: knowing who is part of the community, where they live, and how to reach them makes everything else the parish does more effective. It also tends to surface people who have drifted and bring them back into contact.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Parish Census: Help Us Keep Our Records Current

articles.excerpt: A Schmemann lecture in January 2025 featured a scholar visiting from the United Kingdom, continuing a tradition of theological education in honor of Fr. Alexander Schmemann.
articles.body: A Schmemann lecture in January 2025 featured a scholar visiting from the United Kingdom, continuing a tradition of theological education held in honor of Fr. Alexander Schmemann, whose writing on liturgical theology has shaped Orthodox parishes across North America for decades. The lecture series draws parishioners with an appetite for serious theological engagement, and the international guest brought a perspective rarely heard in the local parish context.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Schmemann Lecture Series: Scholar from the United Kingdom

articles.excerpt: The Induction Ceremony for Rev. Bolivar Flores, rescheduled to January 5th following a family tragedy affecting Bishop Richardson, will be attended by Orthodox clergy and Christian leaders from various traditions.
articles.body: The Induction Ceremony for Rev. Bolivar Flores has been rescheduled to January 5th following the tragedy that affected Bishop Richardson's family. The ceremony will be attended by Bishop Richardson, Orthodox clergy, and Christian leaders from various traditions. Father Solomon will be attending.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Induction Ceremony for Rev. Bolivar Flores

articles.excerpt: The annual Christmas Gala is this Friday, December 20th at 7:00 PM, featuring a concert of Orthodox sacred music and traditional carols in the Cathedral nave. Tickets on Eventbrite.
articles.body: The annual Christmas Gala is this Friday, December 20th at 7:00 PM. Tickets are on Eventbrite; parking across the street is included. Proceeds support church preservation. If cost is a barrier, please speak to Father Solomon privately. Bring friends. The evening includes a concert of Orthodox sacred music and traditional carols in the Cathedral nave, followed by a reception. Flyers are available at the candle stand for posting at work or in public spaces.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Christmas Gala and Concert 2024: Friday, December 20th at 7:00 PM

articles.excerpt: The 2025 Invitation to Stewardship materials are now available. Please take a packet, pray over it, and return your pledge card by January. The Parish Council cannot build a realistic budget without knowing what parishioners intend to give.
articles.body: The 2025 Invitation to Stewardship materials are now available. Archbishop Michael's letter on stewardship has been included. Please take a packet, pray over it, and return your pledge card by January. The Parish Council cannot build a realistic budget without knowing what parishioners intend to give. If you have questions about where the money goes, ask Father Solomon.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: 2025 Stewardship Campaign: Invitation to Pledge

articles.excerpt: This Thursday is the Feast of the Entrance of the Theotokos into the Temple. Note the adjusted Vespers time this Wednesday evening: 6:45 PM rather than the usual time.
articles.body: This Thursday is the Feast of the Entrance of the Theotokos into the Temple. Note the adjusted time: Vespers this Wednesday evening will begin at 6:45 PM (not the usual time) to allow Bible Study to meet first. Divine Liturgy is Thursday morning. Please plan accordingly.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Entrance of the Theotokos into the Temple

articles.excerpt: This Saturday, the Cathedral is open for the annual History Happened Here Walking Tour of Paulus Hook. Volunteers are needed to welcome visitors and explain the iconography and history of the building.
articles.body: This Saturday, the Cathedral is open for the annual "History Happened Here" Walking Tour of Paulus Hook. Tour groups will be coming through the church throughout the day. Parish volunteers are needed to welcome visitors, explain the iconography and history of the building, and distribute information about the Orthodox faith. Sign up to help. Many of these visitors have never been inside an Orthodox church, and what they encounter here matters.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: History Happened Here: Paulus Hook Walking Tour Through the Cathedral

articles.excerpt: A special Parish Assembly will be held this Sunday to address a structural issue: the ceiling above the hall stage is sagging, and the same problem is visible in the nave floor above that area.
articles.body: A special Parish Assembly will be held this Sunday during coffee hour to address a structural issue in the building. The ceiling above the hall stage is sagging, and the same problem is visible in the nave floor above that area. Those sections have been roped off as a precaution. Father Solomon will present the engineering assessment, proposed solutions, and cost estimates. This is a decision that affects everyone; please come and participate.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Special Parish Assembly: Structural Issue in the Building

articles.excerpt: The New Jersey Reentry Corporation is hosting its annual Reentry Cultural Festival this Saturday, October 19th. NJRC helps people returning from incarceration find housing, employment, and community.
articles.body: The New Jersey Reentry Corporation is hosting its annual Reentry Cultural Festival this Saturday, October 19th. NJRC helps people returning from incarceration find housing, employment, and community. The Cathedral has a longstanding relationship with this organization. Come out and show support.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: NJRC Reentry Cultural Festival: Saturday, October 19th

articles.excerpt: Father Solomon will be attending the funeral of Fr. Joseph Chupeck this week. Bible Study is cancelled so he can be present. Please pray for the repose of Father Joseph's soul. Memory eternal.
articles.body: Father Solomon will be attending the funeral of Fr. Joseph Chupeck this week. Bible Study is cancelled so he can be present. Please pray for the repose of Father Joseph's soul and for his family and parish community. Memory eternal.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Repose of Fr. Joseph Chupeck: Memory Eternal

articles.excerpt: The diocesan Fall Teen Retreat is the weekend of October 4th through 6th at Frost Valley YMCA Camp in Claryville, NY. The retreat is free of charge for middle and high school students.
articles.body: The diocesan Fall Teen Retreat is the weekend of October 4th through 6th at Frost Valley YMCA Camp in Claryville, NY, in the Catskills. The retreat is free of charge. Middle and high school students are encouraged to register early so headcounts for housing and meals can be finalized. Contact the Department of Youth Ministry or Father Solomon for registration details.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: OCA Diocese Fall Teen Retreat at Frost Valley in the Catskills

articles.excerpt: Vespers for the Feast of Pokrov is Monday evening, September 30th at 6:30 PM. Divine Liturgy is Tuesday morning, October 1st at 8:30 AM.
articles.body: Vespers for the Feast of Pokrov is Monday evening, September 30th at 6:30 PM. Divine Liturgy is Tuesday morning, October 1st at 8:30 AM. The feast commemorates the 10th-century vision of St. Andrew the Fool-for-Christ, who saw the Virgin Mary spreading her protective veil over the faithful gathered in prayer at the Blachernae Church in Constantinople. These are weekday services; come if you are able.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Protection of the Theotokos (Pokrov)

articles.excerpt: This Sunday at 4 PM, Cappella Romana performs at St. Ignatius Loyola Church on the Upper East Side. Their program recreates the ancient chants of Hagia Sophia and the Byzantine tradition. 13 free tickets available.
articles.body: This Sunday at 4:00 PM, Cappella Romana performs at St. Ignatius Loyola Church on the Upper East Side of Manhattan. Their program recreates the ancient chants of Hagia Sophia and the Byzantine tradition. Father Solomon has called this "a wonderful and rare opportunity." A generous parishioner has offered 13 free tickets to Cathedral members; contact Father Solomon to claim yours. The 4 PM start allows you to attend Divine Liturgy here first before heading to Manhattan.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Cappella Romana at St. Ignatius Loyola: Ancient Chants of Hagia Sophia

articles.excerpt: The NJ District of FOCA is hosting its 2nd Annual Cornhole Tournament and Family Picnic in South River this Saturday, September 28th. Orthodox families from across the region are welcome.
articles.body: The NJ District of FOCA is hosting its 2nd Annual Cornhole Tournament and Family Picnic in South River this Saturday, September 28th. Orthodox families from across the region are welcome. Friendly competition, food, prizes, and a late summer afternoon outside with good people. Registration information is linked below.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: 2nd Annual FOCA Cornhole Tournament and Family Picnic in South River

articles.excerpt: Orthodox Young Adults are gathering this Sunday, September 8th for a Dance Class. No experience required. Open to young adults from any parish.
articles.body: Orthodox Young Adults are gathering this Sunday, September 8th for a Dance Class. No experience required. Open to young adults from any parish. Details on location and time are linked below.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Orthodox Young Adults Dance Class

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral has signed a lease for 107 Grand Street, adding space adjacent to the parish. This expands capacity for ministry and administration in the neighborhood.
articles.body: The Cathedral has signed a lease for 107 Grand Street, adding space adjacent to the parish. This expands our capacity for ministry and administration in the neighborhood. More details on how the space will be used will be shared at the Parish Assembly.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Cathedral Signs Lease for 107 Grand Street

articles.excerpt: This Sunday, Sophie will be received into the Orthodox Church through Chrismation before Divine Liturgy. Following the Liturgy, we will also be celebrating Sophie's birthday.
articles.body: This Sunday, Sophie will be received into the Orthodox Church through Chrismation before Divine Liturgy. Sophie: please come in white, remove your socks before the service for the anointing of feet, and avoid a turtleneck so Father Solomon can reach your collarbone for the chrismation. Following the Liturgy, we will also be celebrating Sophie's birthday. Please come and welcome her into the Church and into the parish family.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Chrismation of Sophie Before Divine Liturgy

articles.excerpt: This Sunday before Divine Liturgy, Noah Kahn and members of the Orsi family will be received as catechumens of the Orthodox Church.

articles.excerpt: This weekend in New York City, the world premiere of Akathist, a new oratorio by Orthodox composer Benedict Sheehan, will be performed. World premieres of serious Orthodox sacred music do not happen often.
articles.body: This weekend in New York City, the world premiere of "Akathist," a new oratorio by Orthodox composer Benedict Sheehan, will be performed. This is a significant new work: a full oratorio setting of one of the most beloved hymns in Orthodox liturgical tradition, composed by one of the leading voices in Orthodox sacred music today. Tickets are available and we strongly encourage interested parishioners to attend. World premieres of serious Orthodox sacred music do not happen often. This is one of them.
