articles.excerpt: The Holy Synod of the Orthodox Church in America has glorified Matushka Olga of Alaska, formally recognizing her as a saint. Her icon will be distributed to parishes in the coming weeks.
articles.body: The Holy Synod of the Orthodox Church in America has glorified Matushka Olga of Alaska, formally recognizing her as a saint. This is a historic moment for the OCA, and for all of us. Matushka Olga served in Alaska's mission field alongside her husband and is remembered for her humility, her tireless service to the poor, and her steadfast faith in genuinely difficult circumstances. Her icon will be distributed to parishes in the coming weeks. She is now one of our intercessors. Memory eternal, and welcome to the calendar.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: The Holy Synod Glorifies Matushka Olga of Alaska

articles.excerpt: A Pan-Orthodox gathering on October 26th recognized Commissioner Sarah Adelman for her service to the community, bringing together Orthodox faithful from multiple jurisdictions.
articles.body: A Pan-Orthodox gathering on October 26th recognized Commissioner Sarah Adelman for her service to the community, bringing together Orthodox faithful from multiple jurisdictions. Gathering across jurisdictional lines to recognize someone's civic contribution is exactly the kind of thing the Pan-Orthodox network in New Jersey does well when it is working.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Pan-Orthodox Recognition of Commissioner Sarah Adelman

articles.excerpt: The second annual Mountain Men's Respite Camping Retreat is this weekend, and there is still time to join. Men are gathering for a weekend of prayer, outdoor Divine Liturgy, and honest conversation.
articles.body: The second annual Mountain Men's Respite Camping Retreat is this weekend, and there is still time to join. Men from across the diocese are gathering for a weekend of prayer, outdoor Divine Liturgy, and honest conversation away from the noise of daily life. Last year's retreat was the kind of weekend people are still talking about, which is why it is happening again. Whether you are looking for spiritual renewal, time in nature, or simply a few days with Orthodox brothers, this retreat is worth the trip. Contact information is below.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Second Annual Mountain Men's Respite Camping Retreat: October 20-22

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral is launching a Young Men's Group for parishioners from mid-teens through mid-twenties. The group meets monthly for fellowship, prayer, and honest conversation about living as an Orthodox man today.
articles.body: The Cathedral is launching a Young Men's Group for parishioners from mid-teens through mid-twenties. The group will meet monthly for fellowship, prayer, and conversation about what it actually means to live as an Orthodox Christian man right now, in this city, at this moment in history. This is not a Bible trivia group. It is a place for honest conversation and real brotherhood. If you are in that age range, or know someone who is, please reach out to Father Solomon for details.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Young Men's Group Launches for Parishioners Ages Mid-Teens to Mid-Twenties

articles.excerpt: The Friends of St. Andrew's Camp met on October 14th to support the diocesan camp program and plan for future seasons. A meeting in October signals that the people involved are in it for the long haul.
articles.body: The Friends of St. Andrew's Camp met on October 14th to support the diocesan camp program and plan for future seasons. Camp ministry requires year-round work from volunteers who care about it, and a meeting in October, well after the summer season has ended, signals that the people involved are in it for the long haul.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Friends of St. Andrew's Camp Ministry Meeting: October 14th

articles.excerpt: Thanks to the generosity of the parish, the fundraiser for new altar server vestments has reached its goal and exceeded it, with the surplus designated toward purchasing ripidia.
articles.body: Thanks to the generosity of the parish, we have successfully funded new vestments for our altar servers. The fundraiser not only reached its goal but exceeded it, and the surplus has been designated toward purchasing ripidia (hexapteryga, the liturgical fans used during hierarchical services). The new vestments and fans will be in use at services going forward. Thank you to everyone who gave. The altar servers serve faithfully every Sunday, and it matters that they are properly equipped to do so.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: New Altar Boy Vestments Funded; Surplus Designated for Ripidia

articles.excerpt: Parish leadership is exploring the possibility of launching ESL classes at the Cathedral. Jersey City is home to many recent immigrants who are working to learn English.
articles.body: Parish leadership is exploring the possibility of launching ESL (English as a Second Language) classes at the Cathedral. Jersey City is home to many recent immigrants who are working to learn English, and a parish-run program could serve both our own community and the neighborhood around us in a concrete and practical way. If you have teaching experience or language skills and would be interested in volunteering for this ministry, please let Father Solomon know. Nothing is finalized yet, but we are taking it seriously.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Parish Leadership Exploring ESL Classes as a New Ministry

articles.excerpt: This Saturday at Holy Resurrection Church in Wayne, a Diocesan Celebration honors Mitered Archpriest Joseph Lickwar and Matushka Shirley on their 60th Wedding Anniversary and his 60th year of ordination.
articles.body: This Saturday, Holy Resurrection Orthodox Church in Wayne, NJ is hosting a Diocesan Celebration honoring Mitered Archpriest Joseph Lickwar and Matushka Shirley on two extraordinary milestones: their 60th Wedding Anniversary and the 60th Anniversary of Fr. Joseph's ordination to the Holy Priesthood. His Eminence Archbishop Michael will preside and lead a Molieben of Thanksgiving. Sixty years of marriage and sixty years of priesthood in the same man is something worth driving to Wayne to celebrate. Details for attending are below.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: 60th Anniversary Celebration for Fr. Joseph and Matushka Shirley Lickwar

articles.excerpt: The New Jersey Reentry Corporation held a food drive on September 26th, and the Cathedral supported it. NJRC works with people returning from incarceration to rebuild stable lives.
articles.body: The New Jersey Reentry Corporation held a food drive on September 26th, and the Cathedral supported it. NJRC works with people returning from incarceration, a population that faces a specific and difficult set of obstacles in rebuilding stable lives. A food drive is a modest contribution to that work, but it is a consistent one.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: NJRC Food Drive: September 26th

articles.excerpt: A Listening for Change community dialogue was held on September 23rd, bringing together neighbors to talk through local concerns and potential solutions.
articles.body: A "Listening for Change" community dialogue was held on September 23rd, bringing together neighbors to talk through local concerns and potential solutions. Community dialogue events are only as good as the willingness of the people in the room to be honest, and parish connections to events like this one signal that the Cathedral sees its neighborhood as something it has a responsibility to.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Listening for Change: Community Dialogue Event

articles.excerpt: The deanery hosted a Young Adults Pig Roast on September 17th, bringing together Orthodox young people from multiple parishes for food and fellowship.
articles.body: The deanery hosted a Young Adults Pig Roast on September 17th, bringing together Orthodox young people from multiple parishes for food and fellowship. A pig roast at a church event is not a subtle gesture, and that is part of what makes it work: it signals a community comfortable enough in itself to do something genuinely festive.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Deanery Young Adults Pig Roast

articles.excerpt: The McGreevey Civic Association held a Back-to-School event on September 16th, providing supplies and support to families heading into the new school year.
articles.body: The McGreevey Civic Association held a Back-to-School event on September 16th, providing supplies and support to families heading into the new school year. The Cathedral shared the event with parishioners as part of its ongoing relationship with civic organizations working in the neighborhood.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: McGreevey Civic Association Back-to-School Event

articles.excerpt: The Diocese of New York and New Jersey held its Fall Teen Retreat from September 15th to 17th, gathering Orthodox teenagers from parishes across the region for worship, teaching, and fellowship.
articles.body: The Diocese of New York and New Jersey held its Fall Teen Retreat from September 15th to 17th, gathering Orthodox teenagers from parishes across the region for worship, teaching, and fellowship. Teen retreats are where a lot of the most durable Orthodox friendships get formed, the kind that carry people through college and into adulthood still connected to the Church.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Diocesan Fall Teen Retreat: September 15-17

articles.excerpt: On the anniversary of the September 11 attacks, the Cathedral held a Lity for those who died that day. The Cathedral is in Jersey City, which lost residents in the attacks and sits across the water from where the towers stood.
articles.body: On the anniversary of the September 11 attacks, the Cathedral held a Lity for those who died on that day in 2001. The Cathedral is in Jersey City, which lost residents in the attacks and sits across the water from where the towers stood. A memorial service on September 11th here is not an abstraction; it is a prayer for neighbors.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Lity for Those Who Died on September 11, 2001

articles.excerpt: Short catechism classes began on September 10th during coffee hour after Sunday Liturgy, providing basic instruction in Orthodox Christian faith and a space for questions.
articles.body: Short catechism classes began on September 10th during coffee hour after Sunday Liturgy, providing basic instruction in Orthodox Christian faith and a space for questions. Starting classes during coffee hour rather than as a separate event lowered the barrier to showing up and let people enter the conversation gradually, which is probably the right approach for a community building this ministry from the ground up.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Short Catechism Classes Launch During Coffee Hour

articles.excerpt: Ambrose was received as a catechumen of the Orthodox Church in September 2023 in a ceremony before Divine Liturgy. The whole parish witnessed his commitment and took on shared responsibility for his journey.
articles.body: Ambrose was received as a catechumen of the Orthodox Church in September 2023 in a ceremony before Divine Liturgy. The rite formally enrolls a person among those preparing for baptism, and it is a public moment: the whole parish witnesses the catechumen's commitment and takes on a shared responsibility for supporting them toward the font.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Making of a Catechumen: Ambrose

articles.excerpt: The parish undertook a study of St. Silouan the Athonite in the fall of 2023, exploring the life and teaching of this 20th-century monk whose writing on humility and prayer has had an outsized influence on modern Orthodox spirituality.
articles.body: The parish undertook a study of St. Silouan the Athonite in the fall of 2023, exploring the life and teaching of this 20th-century monk whose writing on humility, love for enemies, and the prayer of the heart has had an outsized influence on modern Orthodox spirituality. Elder Sophrony's biography of Silouan is one of the books that people read and then cannot stop recommending to others, and studying it together in a parish context tends to go deep.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Parish Study of St. Silouan the Athonite

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral observed the Feast of the Beheading of St. John the Baptist on August 29th. It is a feast day that carries a fast with it; the Church remembers the death of the Forerunner with solemnity.
articles.body: The Cathedral observed the Feast of the Beheading of St. John the Baptist on August 29th. It is a feast day that carries a fast with it; the Church remembers the death of the Forerunner not with celebration but with solemnity. Service times were corrected and communicated to parishioners ahead of the observance.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Beheading of St. John the Baptist

articles.excerpt: The sacrament of Holy Matrimony was celebrated at the Cathedral on August 26th. Every wedding at Sts. Peter and Paul adds to a record of the sacramental life of this parish.
articles.body: The sacrament of Holy Matrimony was celebrated at the Cathedral on August 26th, uniting a couple in the Orthodox Church. Every wedding at Sts. Peter and Paul adds to a record of the sacramental life of a parish that takes seriously its role in marking the most significant moments in the lives of the people who belong to it.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Sacrament of Holy Matrimony at the Cathedral: August 26th

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral celebrated the Dormition of the Theotokos with Great Vespers on August 14th and Divine Liturgy on August 15th. The Dormition concludes the Dormition fast and arrives with the weight of a feast that has been prepared for.
articles.body: The Cathedral celebrated the Dormition of the Theotokos with Great Vespers on August 14th and Divine Liturgy on August 15th. The Dormition, one of the twelve great feasts, commemorates the falling asleep of the Virgin Mary and her bodily assumption into heaven. It concludes the Dormition fast, which runs for the first two weeks of August, and arrives with the weight of a feast that has been prepared for.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Dormition of the Theotokos 2023

articles.excerpt: A pro-life educational event was held on August 13th, offering Orthodox teaching on the sanctity of human life from conception.
articles.body: A pro-life educational event was held on August 13th, offering Orthodox teaching on the sanctity of human life from conception. The Orthodox Church's position on these questions is consistent and rooted in its theological anthropology, and presenting it in an educational format gives parishioners a clearer account of why the Church holds what it holds.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Pro-Life Educational Event: Orthodox Teaching on the Sanctity of Human Life

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral celebrated the Feast of the Transfiguration on August 6th with Divine Liturgy and the traditional blessing of fruit. After the service, Fr. Solomon explained the Transfiguration icon to the children.
articles.body: The Cathedral celebrated the Feast of the Transfiguration on August 6th with Divine Liturgy and the traditional blessing of fruit. After the service, Fr. Solomon explained the Transfiguration icon to the children, walking them through the figures of Moses and Elijah flanking Christ, the disciples falling on their faces, and the uncreated light that the icon tries to make visible. Connecting children to the iconographic tradition through the actual feast is better than any classroom lesson.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Feast of the Transfiguration 2023: Fruit Blessing and Icon Explanation for Children

articles.excerpt: A new Church School program launched on July 23rd, beginning during coffee hour after Divine Liturgy, built around topics including the Divine Liturgy, creation, St. Herman of Alaska, and the Transfiguration.
articles.body: A new Church School program launched on July 23rd, beginning during coffee hour after Divine Liturgy. The curriculum was built around topics including the Divine Liturgy, creation, St. Herman of Alaska, and the Transfiguration, with volunteers coordinating to keep it running week to week. Launching a Church School in the summer, before the fall rush, gave it time to find its footing before the calendar got crowded.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: New Church School Program Launches During Coffee Hour

articles.excerpt: The parish sang Many Years (Polychronion) for a child's name day on July 30th. In Orthodox practice, name days honor the saint whose name a person bears, marking them in the context of the Liturgy.
articles.body: The parish sang "Many Years" (Polychronion) for a child's name day on July 30th during or after Divine Liturgy. In Orthodox practice, name days honor the saint whose name a person bears, and marking them in the context of the Liturgy connects the celebration directly to the Church's prayer rather than keeping it as a private family occasion.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Name Day Celebration During Divine Liturgy

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral celebrated its patronal feast on June 29th with Vespers and June 30th with Divine Liturgy, followed by a potluck celebration. This was Fr. Solomon's first patronal feast as the Cathedral's priest.
articles.body: The Cathedral celebrated its patronal feast on June 29th with Vespers and June 30th with Divine Liturgy, followed by a potluck celebration. This was Fr. Solomon's first patronal feast as the Cathedral's priest, which gave the 2023 celebration a particular significance. The patronal feast is the most important day in the parish year, the one the rest of the calendar orients around.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Patronal Feast of Saints Peter and Paul 2023: Fr. Solomon's First as Rector

articles.excerpt: Fr. Solomon Longo arrived as the Cathedral's new priest on June 1st, 2023, marking the beginning of stable, continuous pastoral leadership after a period of transition.
articles.body: Fr. Solomon Longo arrived as the Cathedral's new priest on June 1st, 2023. His arrival followed a period of transition in which the parish had been served by visiting clergy, and it marked the beginning of stable, continuous pastoral leadership. Most of what is documented in this record was made possible by the foundation laid in the months after he arrived. June 1st is a date the Cathedral will likely mark for years.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Fr. Solomon Longo Arrives as the Cathedral's New Priest

articles.excerpt: In 2023, the parish participated in outreach to recently arrived Ukrainian refugees, helping connect them with information about TB screening and other health services.
articles.body: In 2023, the parish participated in outreach to recently arrived Ukrainian refugees, helping connect them with information about TB screening and other health services. Jersey City received a significant number of Ukrainian arrivals following Russia's invasion, and the Cathedral's outreach to that community reflects both a pastoral instinct and a practical recognition that many of those arrivals share the Orthodox faith.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Outreach to Ukrainian Arrivals: TB Screening and Health Services

articles.excerpt: A BBQ fundraiser was held before Memorial Day 2023 to support St. Andrew's Camp, the Diocese's summer camp for Orthodox youth. Archbishop Michael encouraged parish support for the camp.
articles.body: A BBQ fundraiser was held before Memorial Day 2023 to support St. Andrew's Camp, the Diocese's summer camp for Orthodox youth. Archbishop Michael encouraged parish support for the camp, which has served generations of young Orthodox Christians in the Northeast. A BBQ in May is also just a good reason to gather, and combining fellowship with a purpose tends to make both better.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: BBQ Fundraiser in Support of St. Andrew's Camp

articles.excerpt: The Historic Paulus Hook Association held a Pancake Breakfast fundraiser at the Cathedral on April 22nd, with church tours and Orthodox faith pamphlets distributed to curious visitors.
articles.body: The Historic Paulus Hook Association held a Pancake Breakfast fundraiser at the Cathedral on April 22nd. The event included church tours and distribution of pamphlets about the Orthodox faith to visitors who came for breakfast and stayed curious. A Pancake Breakfast that turns into an informal introduction to Orthodoxy for curious neighbors is a pretty good return on a morning of cooking.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: HPHA Pancake Breakfast at the Cathedral with Church Tours

articles.excerpt: The Cathedral observed the full cycle of Holy Week services in April 2023, including Holy Unction on Holy Wednesday. This was Fr. Solomon's first Holy Week as the Cathedral's priest.
articles.body: The Cathedral observed the full cycle of Holy Week services in April 2023, including Holy Unction on Holy Wednesday and the sacred services leading to Pascha. Volunteers worked to beautify the church ahead of the services, a preparation that has its own meaning: making the space ready for the most important week of the year is itself an act of worship. This was Fr. Solomon's first Holy Week as the Cathedral's priest.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Holy Week Services 2023: Fr. Solomon's First Holy Week at Sts. Peter and Paul

articles.excerpt: An Orthodox Lenten Concert featuring the music of Arvo Part was held during Great Lent 2023, with Metropolitan Nicholas expected to attend.
articles.body: An Orthodox Lenten Concert featuring the music of Arvo Part was held during Great Lent 2023, with Metropolitan Nicholas expected to attend. Part is the Estonian composer whose tintinnabuli style of sacred music has become one of the most listened-to bodies of work in contemporary classical music, and performing it in a Lenten context, inside a functioning Orthodox church, is the setting it was written for.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: Orthodox Lenten Concert: The Music of Arvo Part

articles.excerpt: During 2021 and 2022, Saints Peter and Paul Cathedral was served by Mitered Archpriest Joseph Lickwar, who also carried the responsibilities of Chancellor of the Diocese of New York and New Jersey.
articles.body: During 2021 and 2022, Saints Peter and Paul Cathedral was served by Mitered Archpriest Joseph Lickwar, who also carried the responsibilities of Chancellor of the Diocese of New York and New Jersey. Fr. Lickwar brought to the Cathedral decades of experience and steady pastoral care at a moment when the parish needed continuity. The full liturgical calendar was maintained throughout this period, with services celebrated every Saturday evening and Sunday morning. Fr. Lickwar's presence here during this chapter of the Cathedral's life was a gift, and his 60th anniversary of ordination in 2023 is a fitting occasion to mark it.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: The Cathedral Under Mitered Archpriest Joseph Lickwar: 2021-2022

articles.excerpt: Through 2021 and 2022, the Cathedral maintained its full liturgical schedule. Every major feast was celebrated. Saturday Vespers, Sunday Liturgy, week after week. That faithfulness is the foundation Fr. Solomon arrived to build on.
articles.body: Through 2021 and 2022, the Cathedral maintained its full liturgical schedule. Every major feast was celebrated. Saturday Vespers at 5:00 PM, Sunday Liturgy at 9:30 AM, week after week. Visiting clergy and Fr. Lickwar kept the rhythm going. This is worth noting because it is not a given: parishes in transition can drift, attendance can thin, people can lose the habit. That did not happen here. The community showed up, and the services were served. That faithfulness is the foundation Fr. Solomon arrived to build on in June 2023.
articles.author: Parish Office
articles.title: A Parish in Transition: Maintaining Liturgical Life Through 2021-2022
